_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, ophthalmic examination
Irritation in the affected eye and foreign body sensation

preauth.label: Clinical photograph of the affected part
Full face picture of the patient

claim.label: Still image of the patient undergoing the procedure with patient ID and date
Other essential investigations- Blood Pressure & Blood Sugar (Fasting, PP and Random)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes detailing history of trauma (+/-), clinical symptoms and decrease in visual acuity?
Clinical photo of Affected part with Proper labelling of Affected Eye whether R or L with full face photograph.
Did the report suggest decrease in visual acuity?

cpd_claim.q: Are Blood Sugar (fasting, PP and/or random) investigations done and reports available if patient age >40years or patient is a known diabetic?
Do OT notes detail the steps of surgery performed and was the surgery successful?
Are the documents available to show appropriate post-op care, advise including for follow-up?
Was the Pterygium excision and conjunctival autograft done in the same eye?

common_queries: Report does not suggest a decrease in visual acuity, or acuity not documented.
Clinical photograph of affected part is missing, improperly labelled (R/L), or full face picture of patient is missing.
Pterygium excision and conjunctival autograft not performed on the same eye.
Blood Sugar (fasting, PP and/or random) reports missing for patients >40 years or known diabetics.
Operative notes do not detail surgical steps or success.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Pterygium + Conjunctival Autograft Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, ophthalmic examination
Irritation in the affected eye and foreign body sensation
